text: The robotic arms are used to perform a surgeon's knot with a double loop at the wrist junction, preventing slippage of the loops. The left arm grabs the end of the tie on the screen's right side.

The robotic arms manipulate the tie, with the right arm grabbing a distal portion to pull through the knot. As the knot is tightened, one end of the tie is pulled vertically to maintain visibility and facilitate efficient knot completion, positioning the tie for the next throw.
The surgeon completes the third and final tie using alternating hands with robotic arms, ensuring the instruments remain visible and avoiding collisions or excessive force.
